file_analyses.file_purpose: Utility module containing the CommonUtilities class which provides shared helper methods for HP Smart Windows app automation using Appium WebDriver. Contains methods for clicking elements with explicit waits, handling Chrome browser sign-in flows via keyboard automation using pynput, switching between application contexts, checking printer status, adding printers if not present, and executing print document workflows with full job status validation.

imports.import_statement: from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC

classes.class_purpose: Utility class providing shared helper methods for HP Smart Windows app automation. Contains reusable methods for element interaction with explicit waits, Chrome browser sign-in automation using pynput keyboard controller, application context switching, printer status checking and automatic printer addition if not present, and complete print document workflow execution with job status validation from file sending through job completion notification.

parameters.name: driver
parameters.description: Appium WebDriver instance for HP Smart Windows app automation

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the CommonUtilities class by storing the provided Appium WebDriver instance as self.driver. This driver reference is used by all subsequent methods in the class for element location, interaction, and wait operations. The driver must be an active Appium session connected to the HP Smart Windows app via WinAppDriver.

parameters.name: type_of_locator
parameters.description: Appium locator type (e.g., AppiumBy.NAME, AppiumBy.XPATH, AppiumBy.ACCESSIBILITY_ID)

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: Locator value string to identify the element

parameters.name: time
parameters.description: Timeout in seconds to wait for element to be clickable. Defaults to 30 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Waits for an element to become clickable and then clicks it using Selenium WebDriverWait with expected_conditions. Creates a WebDriverWait instance with self.driver and the provided timeout parameter. Uses EC.element_to_be_clickable with a tuple of (type_of_locator, element) to wait until the element is both visible and enabled. Once the element is clickable, immediately calls .click() on it. This is a core reusable method for all element click operations in HP Smart app automation.

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: Locator value string to identify the Accept All button

functions.what_it_does: Waits for the Accept All button element to be present in the HP Smart app and then clicks it. Uses WebDriverWait with a 150-second timeout and EC.presence_of_element_located to wait for the element identified by the provided locator tuple (type_of_locator, element). Once the element is located, immediately calls .click() on it. This method is specifically designed to handle the HP Smart app's initial privacy/terms acceptance dialog that appears on first launch.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: HP account email address for sign-in

functions.what_it_does: Automates the HP account sign-in process in a Chrome browser window using pynput keyboard controller for keyboard input simulation. Waits 30 seconds for the sign-in page to load, then uses keyboard.pressed(Key.ctrl) context manager to select all text with Ctrl+A, presses backspace to clear any existing text, types the username string using keyboard.type(), waits 10 seconds, presses Enter to submit the username form, waits 15 seconds for the password page to load, types the password string, waits 15 seconds, presses Enter to submit the password form, waits 15 seconds for authentication to complete, then attempts to press left arrow and Enter keys to handle any additional prompts. All keyboard operations use the pynput Controller instance stored in the module-level keyboard variable. Includes exception handling that prints error messages if HP Smart is not visible.

Switches the Appium driver context back to the HP Smart Windows app window. Retrieves the HP Smart app session ID using self.driver.session_id and stores it in the hpsmart variable, then calls self.driver.switch_to.window(hpsmart) to switch the driver focus to that window handle. This method is used after interacting with other windows (such as Chrome browser for sign-in) to return control to the HP Smart app for continued automation.

Checks whether a printer has been successfully added to the HP Smart app by waiting for the NextDeviceButton element to be present. Uses WebDriverWait with a 30-second timeout and EC.presence_of_element_located to search for a Button element with AutomationId 'NextDeviceButton' using an XPath locator. If the element is found within the timeout, the method completes successfully indicating a printer is added. If the element is not found, a TimeoutException is caught and the method prints 'Printer not added' along with the exception details. This is a verification method that does not return a boolean but uses exception handling to indicate status.

Checks if a printer is already added to HP Smart by looking for a 'Ready' status element, and if not found, automatically initiates the add printer workflow. First uses WebDriverWait with 30-second timeout to search for an element with Name 'Ready' which indicates a printer is already added and online. If this element is not found (TimeoutException), prints 'Printer not added' and then attempts to click an 'Add Printer' button using self.click_element with a 150-second timeout. If the Add Printer button is not visible, catches the exception and prints 'Add Printer not visible'. Then waits for the printer search process by looking for 'Searching for printers...' text with a 20-second timeout, followed by waiting up to 50 seconds for the invitation message 'You've been invited to use this printer'. Once found, clicks the 'Online' element to select the printer, then waits up to 50 seconds for the 'Ready' status to appear confirming successful printer addition. If the printer is not found during the search, catches the exception and prints 'Printer not found'. This is a composite action method that combines verification and conditional setup logic.

Executes a complete print document workflow in the HP Smart app from selecting a sample document through job completion notification validation. Clicks the 'Print Documents' button using self.click_element with AppiumBy.NAME locator, waits 5 seconds, switches to the first window handle using self.driver.switch_to.window(self.driver.window_handles[0]), attempts to click a 'Sample' document element, then clicks the 'Print' button. Switches back to the first window handle and waits for 'Sample.pdf' element to appear with a 30-second timeout, then clicks 'Print' button twice in succession with debug print statements between each click. After initiating the print job, waits for a sequence of status elements to appear: 'Sending File' (30s timeout), 'Cancel' button (30s), 'File Sent!' message (30s), 'OK' button (30s), then clicks the 'Job Status' element. Waits 5 seconds, switches to first window handle, waits 5 seconds, then waits for 'Print Job Processing...' text (30s), waits for 'Job Printed' status and clicks it (30s), waits for 'Clear Notification' button and clicks it (30s), waits 5 seconds, then switches to the second window handle using self.driver.window_handles[1]. If any exception occurs during the print job status validation sequence, raises a new Exception with message 'Print Job failed!' and includes the original exception. Multiple try-except blocks handle different failure points with specific print statements for debugging.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: CommonUtilities is a utility class that uses Appium WebDriver for HP Smart Windows app automation. It must be instantiated with an active appium_driver fixture. The class provides a mix of Appium-based element interaction methods (click_element, click_accept_all) and pynput-based keyboard automation methods (signin_chrome). The print_documents method is a complex composite action that orchestrates the entire print workflow including window switching, status validation, and notification handling. The module defines a module-level keyboard Controller instance and data_variables dict containing test configuration data including printer IP, PIN, and test account credentials. This class does not define any Appium tuple locators at the class level — all locators are passed as parameters to methods.

file_analyses.file_purpose: pytest configuration file defining fixtures and hooks for HP Smart Windows app test automation. Contains the appium_driver session-scoped fixture that initializes an Appium WebDriver connection to the HP Smart Windows app via WinAppDriver, maximizes the app window, yields the driver to tests, and quits the driver after the test session completes. Also includes a helper function minAllApps() to minimize all open windows using PowerShell and commented-out fixture code for Chrome browser automation.

functions.what_it_does: Minimizes all open application windows on the Windows desktop using a PowerShell command. Constructs a PowerShell command string that creates a Shell.Application COM object and calls its MinimizeAll() method. Executes this command using subprocess.run with the powershell executable, passing the command via the -Command parameter. Captures both stdout and stderr output as text using capture_output=True and text=True parameters. This is a standalone utility function (not a class method) used to clear the desktop before launching HP Smart app tests to ensure a clean test environment.

pytest session-scoped fixture that initializes and provides an Appium WebDriver instance for HP Smart Windows app automation. Assigns the desired_caps_hp_smart dictionary to the desired_caps variable, then creates a webdriver.Remote instance connecting to the local WinAppDriver server at 'http://127.0.0.1:4723/' using AppiumOptions().load_capabilities(desired_caps) to load the capabilities. Stores the driver in a global driver variable and also as a local variable. Calls driver.maximize_window() to maximize the HP Smart app window. Yields the driver instance to the test function, allowing the test to use it. After the test session completes (after yield), calls driver.quit() to close the HP Smart app and terminate the Appium session. This fixture is used by all HP Smart app tests that require Appium automation.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This conftest.py file is the pytest configuration for HP Smart Windows app tests. The appium_driver fixture is session-scoped meaning it creates one Appium session for the entire test run and all tests share the same HP Smart app instance. The desired_caps_hp_smart dictionary specifies the HP Smart app package ID 'AD2F1837.HPPrinterControl_v10z8vjag6ke6!AD2F1837.HPPrinterControl' which WinAppDriver uses to launch the app. The fixture connects to WinAppDriver running on localhost port 4723. Tests must use this fixture by either declaring it as a parameter or using @pytest.mark.usefixtures('appium_driver'). The file also contains commented-out code for Chrome browser fixture and pytest hooks for JSON reporting which are not currently active.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Test script file for HP Smart Windows app automation tests. Currently contains only commented-out test code. The commented test function test_hp_smart_sign_in demonstrates the intended test structure using pytest markers, the appium_driver fixture, and CommonUtilities class methods for clicking Accept All button, signing in via Chrome, checking printer status, and executing print document workflows.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This file is entirely commented out and contains no active code. The commented code shows the intended test pattern: a test function decorated with @pytest.mark.checking and @pytest.mark.usefixtures('appium_driver'), retrieving the appium_driver fixture using request.getfixturevalue(), setting implicit wait, and calling CommonUtilities methods like click_accept_all(), click_element(), signin_chrome(), printer_status_check_add_if_not_added(), and print_documents(). The commented code also shows alternative approaches using WebDriverWait and find_elements for clicking Accept All button and using pynput keyboard controller for tab navigation. Since all code is commented, this file contributes no active classes, methods, locators, or imports to the framework.

file_analyses.file_purpose: pytest configuration file for HP Smart web portal (SMB portal) tests using Selenium Chrome WebDriver. Currently entirely commented out. The commented code defines a chrome_browser module-scoped fixture that would initialize a Chrome WebDriver instance using webdriver_manager to automatically download and manage ChromeDriver, set Chrome options to start maximized, navigate to the HP SMB stage portal URL, yield the driver to tests, and quit the driver after tests complete.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This file is entirely commented out and contains no active code. The commented fixture chrome_browser would provide Selenium Chrome WebDriver automation for HP Smart web portal tests at https://smb.stage.portalshell.int.hp.com/. The fixture uses webdriver_manager's ChromeDriverManager to automatically handle ChromeDriver binary downloads and version management. The fixture is module-scoped meaning one browser instance per test module. Since all code is commented, this file contributes no active fixtures, classes, methods, or imports to the framework.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Test script file for HP Smart web portal (SMB portal) automation tests using Selenium Chrome WebDriver. Currently entirely commented out. The commented code demonstrates the intended test structure for SMB portal sign-in and navigation tests, including a chrome_browser fixture definition and a test_smb test function that automates the login flow by entering username and password, clicking sign-in, accepting cookies, and navigating to the Printers section.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This file is entirely commented out and contains no active code. The commented code shows two versions of the same test pattern: a chrome_browser fixture that initializes Selenium Chrome WebDriver and navigates to https://smb.stage.portalshell.int.hp.com/, and a test_smb function that uses WebDriverWait with Selenium By locators (By.ID, By.XPATH) to automate the SMB portal login flow. The test waits for username field (ID 'username'), enters email, clicks submit button (ID 'user-name-form-submit'), enters password (ID 'password'), clicks sign-in button (ID 'sign-in'), accepts cookies (ID 'onetrust-accept-btn-handler'), and clicks Printers link (XPATH with text 'Printers'). Since all code is commented, this file contributes no active classes, methods, locators, or imports to the framework.

key_reusable_methods: CommonUtilities.click_element — Waits for element to be clickable with WebDriverWait and clicks it
CommonUtilities.click_accept_all — Waits for Accept All button and clicks it with 150s timeout
CommonUtilities.signin_chrome — Automates HP account sign-in in Chrome using pynput keyboard controller
CommonUtilities.switch_to_hpsmart — Switches Appium driver context back to HP Smart app window
CommonUtilities.printer_status_check_add_if_not_added — Checks printer status and adds printer if not present
CommonUtilities.print_documents — Executes complete print workflow from document selection through job completion
minAllApps — Minimizes all Windows desktop windows using PowerShell command
appium_driver — pytest session fixture that initializes Appium WebDriver for HP Smart app
